title: Re-express existing checks as default rules (hard migration)
description:
Re-express the complete set of existing hard-coded checks as default rules and remove them from validation.rs and validate.rs.

## Background

For a single source of truth, all current enforcement becomes `rules.toml` content. Behavior must be identical, preserving today's warn-versus-reject semantics via the `enforce` flag, EXCEPT for the one documented deviation below. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` ("Migration", step 8) and decision record section 8.3.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] Default rules cover the full set: type-label requirement, label format, namespace registry, orphan-leaf warning, strategic-label consistency, and per-namespace allowed-values, value-pattern, uniqueness, and required.
- [ ] [hard] Default rules preserve today's warn-vs-reject behavior via `enforce` (default false), modulo the approved deviation in "## Approved Deviations"; parity is covered by tests comparing old and new outcomes.
- [ ] [hard] The hard-coded checks are removed from `validation.rs` (IssueValidator) and `validate.rs` (`validate_labels`/type-hierarchy); no legacy enforcement remains in code.

## Approved Deviations

- **Custom `validation.label_regex` now also applies to `jit validate`** (user-approved 2026-06-07). Legacy `validate_labels` checked only the FIXED canonical regex on the validate path and applied a custom `label_regex` write-time only (gated by `reject_malformed_labels`). The rule engine has no "write-only" representation (a write-blocking rule needs `severity=error`, and the validate path fails on any `severity=error` finding regardless of `enforce`; decision record section 7 gives local rules no write-only carve-out). Migrating the custom regex therefore makes it apply to BOTH paths. The new behavior is stricter and consistent across write and validate. This repo is unaffected because its `label_regex` equals the canonical regex (so the `default:label-format-custom` rule is not emitted).

## Notes

Anchors: `validation.rs:12-160`; `validate.rs` `validate_labels` 279-408 and `validate_type_hierarchy` 410-449; `config.rs` ValidationConfig 65-84 and NamespaceConfig 140-159.
